sell-on-korean-marketplaces.title: How to Sell on Korean Marketplaces — a Structured Overview
sell-on-korean-marketplaces.desc: The main routes for foreign sellers and brands into Korean e-commerce: marketplace types, what each requires, and where logistics and payments fit.
sell-on-korean-marketplaces.intro: Korea is one of the world's largest e-commerce markets, but almost all platform information is published in Korean only. This guide maps the landscape by structure — what kinds of platforms exist and how they differ — so you can shortlist channels before engaging local partners. It describes process, not legal or tax advice.

steps.d: General open marketplaces reach the widest audience and typically require a Korean business registration; vertical malls (fashion, food, handmade) curate sellers more tightly but convert better in their niche.
Korea has strong social commerce — group-buying apps and gift commerce move high volume at low price points. These suit consumer products with clear price appeal.

Same-day and next-day delivery is the consumer baseline in Korea. Most foreign sellers use a local fulfillment partner or a marketplace's own logistics program rather than shipping cross-border per order.

steps.t: Check entry requirements on official sites
steps.d: Fee schedules, settlement cycles, and seller eligibility change frequently and differ by category. Always verify on each platform's official seller page — this directory links to every official site and does not republish fee tables in English.

sell-on-korean-marketplaces.note: Common entry barriers to research before committing: Korean business registration (사업자등록), product safety certification (KC) for many categories, and Korean-language customer service expectations. Most foreign brands enter via a local distributor, agency, or cross-border program first.

fulfillment-and-logistics-korea.title: Fulfillment & Logistics for Selling in Korea
fulfillment-and-logistics-korea.desc: How order fulfillment works in Korean e-commerce and the platform categories that handle storage, packing, shipping, and cross-border flows.
fulfillment-and-logistics-korea.intro: Korean consumers expect delivery speed that is hard to meet from overseas warehouses. This page explains the structural options — marketplace logistics programs, third-party fulfillment, and cross-border services — and links to the platforms in each category.

steps.d: Some large marketplaces operate their own fulfillment programs: you inbound stock to their warehouses and they handle storage, delivery, and returns. Fastest consumer experience, tightest requirements.
Independent fulfillment providers store inventory and ship to any channel you sell on — useful when selling across several marketplaces at once.
For test-selling without local inventory, cross-border programs and trade platforms connect overseas sellers with Korean demand, at slower delivery speeds.

fulfillment-and-logistics-korea.note: Delivery speed expectations vary by category — fresh food and daily necessities are effectively same/next-day markets, while niche imports tolerate longer lead times.

korean-ecommerce-overview.title: Korean E-commerce: a Platform Landscape Overview
korean-ecommerce-overview.desc: A structural map of Korea's online commerce platforms — marketplaces, verticals, social commerce, wholesale, and the supporting infrastructure.
korean-ecommerce-overview.intro: This directory catalogs 1,600+ Korean business platforms in a single 45-category taxonomy. The English layer covers the commerce and trade categories most relevant to foreign sellers. Each entry links to the official site; details like fees and eligibility should always be checked there.

steps.d: The high-traffic center of Korean e-commerce — open marketplaces, price-comparison-driven malls, and app-first players.
Category-specialist channels with curated sellers: fashion, beauty, food & fresh, handmade, kids, and more.
B2B platforms where retailers source inventory — also the route many foreign brands use to reach many Korean retailers at once.
If you run your own Korean web store instead of (or alongside) marketplaces: hosted mall builders and the fulfillment layer behind them.

korean-ecommerce-overview.note: The full directory (including services, funding, content, and AI tools categories) is currently Korean-only. Partnership matching and the business-transfer board serve Korea-based businesses in Korean, under Korean law.
